title: Why I Changed My Mind on Saunas

chunks.title: Introduction to Sauna Benefits
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the different types of saunas—traditional versus infrared—and the benefits they present, particularly in stress management. He notes that while saunas can be useful, overuse might negate their benefits, especially for those already under physical stress.
chunks.transcript: What's up, man? So, I have like a little bit of a three-part question. Um, I do a lot of content and I like to introduce introduce a lot of people to cold plunge, sauna, contrast therapy. I'd love to know what your take is on all three, but a little more specifically on saunas, traditional versus infrared. I think that all of us have some sort of allstatic load, which is this idea that like we all carry a stress load, and saunas are stressful. chunks.title: Detoxification and Longevity Benefits
chunks.summary: The speaker elaborates on the idea of detoxification through sauna use and suggests that the true longevity benefits might depend on existing exercise habits. He emphasizes that beginners may see drastic benefits, but those already active might not gain significantly from saunas.
chunks.transcript: you're already doing jiu-jitsu and hitting the punching bag and doing hit workouts and throwing kettle bells around and swimming and maybe not sleeping a ton because your kids are young or something and you try and throw sauna on top of that, you're just not going to get a net benefit. I don't think for humans. Um, I think that there are unique benefits to sauna perhaps in terms of detoxification, but I don't think there's anything magical about it, right? I think the Finnish studies that we see for sauna are like, you know, it's interesting for me to think how much exercise are these other are these guys getting in Finland and are these humans that are just basically the only exercise they're getting is the sauna. Well, yes. If you're going from zero to one and you're getting the equivalent of a few walks per week, you're going to see a massive longevity benefit to sauna. Does that mean sauna benefits you right in terms of longevity? If you are already lifting weights, if you're already doing, you know, if you're already running six or 60 miles a week, it's a hard call. And I think we have to be honest about that. chunks.title: Heart Rate Variability and Sauna Effects
chunks.summary: The importance of heart rate variability (HRV) is discussed as a measure of readiness and recovery in the context of sauna use. The speaker shares personal experiences with HRV changes due to sauna and exercise routines.

chunks.title: Cold Plunge and Contrast Therapy
chunks.summary: The speaker enjoys combining sauna use with cold plunging for its positive effects on mood and neurotransmitters. He recommends specific protocols for using these therapies together while highlighting the importance of being mindful of long-term stress on the body.

chunks.title: Infrared Light and Overall Health
chunks.summary: The discussion turns to the importance of infrared light for human health and the potential deficiency caused by spending too much time indoors. The speaker emphasizes how infrared light plays a role in melatonin production and overall wellness.

chunks.title: Sauna and Cold Plunge Safety Considerations
chunks.summary: The final segment addresses the risks associated with contrast therapy, particularly for individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions. The speaker advises caution and listening to one's own body when engaging in these practices.
chunks.transcript: >> Totally. And would you say that time and temperature would absolutely make the difference? >> Absolutely. Yeah. We use our sweat glands to do a lot a lot of detoxification and we want to sweat. I just think be careful.
